title: Georgetown Steam Plant - City Light | seattle.gov
content: # Georgetown Steam Plant. The Georgetown Steam Plant is a National Historic Landmark — a reminder of the era of electrification when industry was first attracted to Seattle by its inexpensive hydroelectric power and electric trolley car system. Once at the center of the rapidly growing Georgetown neighborhood, the Georgetown Steam Plant was built by the Seattle Electric Company in 1906-1907 on 18 acres of land along the Duwamish River. The plant's two vertical Curtis Turbines helped establish the steam turbine as capable of producing large amounts of power more cheaply and efficiently than other generators at the time. You can also take a 360-degree virtual tour of the Georgetown Steam Plant by clicking the button below. This virtual tour is presented by Seattle City Light in partnership with the Georgetown Steam Plant Community Development Authority. To enhance your visit - or if you can't join us in person - get an inside view of the history and engineering of the Georgetown Steam Plant:. * Inside the Georgetown Steam Plant (video).
